input: I've been a bit more tired than usual lately. I'm 28 and generally healthy.
rationale: Single mild symptom, young healthy adult, no risk factors

input: I have dry itchy skin on my legs. It's been like this for a few days.
rationale: Low-weight symptom, short duration, non-specific to diabetes

input: I'm 45 years old and have type 2 diabetes. My sugar has been stable around 140. Just need a refill on metformin.
rationale: Known diabetic, stable, no acute symptoms, routine care need

input: I've been feeling a little tired and thirsty today. I think I just didn't drink enough water. I'm 30.
rationale: Mild symptoms with situational explanation, young age, single day

input: I'm 52 and I've been peeing a lot and feeling really thirsty for about 2 weeks now. I also feel exhausted all the time.
rationale: Classic triad of diabetes symptoms, 2+ weeks duration, age >35

input: I'm 38, my mom has diabetes. For the past week I've had blurry vision and I keep needing to go to the bathroom. I've also lost about 8 pounds without trying.
rationale: Multiple diabetes-specific symptoms, family history, unexplained weight loss

input: I'm 65, I have diabetes and I stopped taking my medication two weeks ago because I ran out. Now I'm peeing constantly and my vision is blurry.
rationale: Known diabetic, medication non-adherence, age amplifier, active symptoms

input: I'm 34 and pregnant, about 26 weeks. I've been much more thirsty than usual and urinating frequently. My feet are also tingling.
rationale: Pregnancy with diabetes-like symptoms, gestational diabetes risk window

input: I've had a cut on my foot for 3 weeks and it won't heal. I also get these recurring yeast infections. I'm 48 and overweight.
rationale: Multiple diabetes indicators, prolonged healing, risk factors present

input: My son is 14 and diabetic. He's been vomiting all day, his breath smells fruity, and he seems confused. His blood sugar reads 380.
rationale: Classic DKA presentation: vomiting, fruity breath, confusion, high glucose. Medical emergency.

input: I'm 72 with type 2 diabetes. My blood sugar is over 500 and I can barely think straight. I feel very confused and extremely dehydrated.
rationale: Probable HHS: glucose >500, altered mental status, severe dehydration, elderly

input: I have diabetes and my sugar dropped to 45. I'm shaking badly and feel like I might pass out. I'm alone at home.
rationale: Severe hypoglycemia (<54 mg/dL) with near-syncope, alone and unable to self-rescue

input: I'm having chest pain and trouble breathing. I'm 58 and diabetic. The pain started an hour ago.
rationale: Chest pain in diabetic patient — possible MI. Emergency regardless of glucose level.

input: My grandmother is 80, has diabetes, and she just had a seizure. She's unconscious now. Her blood sugar was 50 before the seizure.
rationale: Severe hypoglycemic event with seizure and loss of consciousness. Call 911.

input: I'm breathing really fast and deep, I can't stop. I feel nauseous and my stomach hurts badly. I'm type 1 diabetic and I ran out of insulin 2 days ago.
rationale: Kussmaul breathing, GI symptoms, insulin omission in T1DM — classic DKA presentation

input: I'm 42 and I've noticed numbness in both feet that's been getting worse over the past month. I also have some tingling in my fingers. No diabetes diagnosis but my father had it.
rationale: Progressive neuropathy symptoms, family history, needs evaluation for diabetes

input: I was diagnosed with prediabetes last year. A1C was 5.9. I've been exercising and eating better. Just checking in about what else I should do.
rationale: Known prediabetes, actively managing with lifestyle, no acute symptoms

input: I'm 55 and I've been extremely hungry lately even though I eat normal meals. I've also been losing weight — about 10 pounds in the last month. And I'm always thirsty.
rationale: Classic diabetes triad with significant unexplained weight loss

input: I just checked my blood sugar and it says 320. I've been throwing up since this morning and I feel terrible. I'm diabetic and took my insulin yesterday but forgot today.
rationale: Glucose >300 with vomiting + insulin omission — high DKA risk, needs emergency evaluation
